summary: The Dockerized reproduction builds the vulnerable Soft Serve commit, starts a local server, and shows that a normal authenticated user can import an admin-only on-disk repository path and recover the secret content from the stolen clone.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should allow low-privileged `user1` to import the server-local path of `admin-secret.git` into `stolen` and recover the proof string `TOP-SECRET-FROM-ADMIN` from the unauthorized clone.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned upstream commit `85e19f1efe706115c1a5a16820b25b8ea61d6056`, built the reproduction image, started the vulnerable server, created the private `admin-secret` repo, pushed `secret.txt`, ran `repo import --lfs-endpoint https://example.com stolen ${DATA_PATH}/repos/admin-secret.git` as `user1`, then cloned `stolen` and printed `TOP-SECRET-FROM-ADMIN`, followed by `[evohunt] verification passed: imported repo exposed TOP-SECRET-FROM-ADMIN` and `[evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified`.
verification_method: The container-side verifier builds the vulnerable binary, drives the SSH and Git workflow end to end, then compares `git -C stolen-copy show HEAD:secret.txt` against the expected proof string. The run only passes if the unauthorized import succeeds and the stolen repository contains the admin-only secret.
evidence_produced_by_run: The reproduced workflow created `admin-secret`, pushed `secret.txt`, and completed `repo import --lfs-endpoint https://example.com stolen ${DATA_PATH}/repos/admin-secret.git` as `user1`.
The final `git -C stolen-copy show HEAD:secret.txt` output was exactly `TOP-SECRET-FROM-ADMIN`.
The run ended with `[evohunt] verification passed: imported repo exposed TOP-SECRET-FROM-ADMIN` and `[evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
